original: e) “RIGHTS” shall mean all of the endorsement rights, services and other rights and benefits granted to Berkshire in this Agreement.
modified:
Clause:
e) "BENEFITS" shall encompass all endorsement privileges, services, and other rights and advantages bestowed upon the Endorsee (you) in accordance with this Agreement.

This rewritten clause shifts the responsibility of the rights to the endorsee (you), whereas in the original clause, Berkshire held those rights.

original: g) “BERKSHIRE COMPETITOR ” is any person or entity that in any way competes with Berkshire ’s financial services.
ollama_generated: h) "Competing Entities" encompass any individual or organization engaged in the provision of financial services, who could potentially pose a competitive threat to Berkshire's operations within the same market sector.

original: Source: BERKSHIRE HILLS BANCORP INC, 10-Q, 8/9/2012 3. EXCLUSIVITY.  Auriemma expressly agrees and undertakes that:
modified:
Clause:
Altered: BERKSHIRE HILLS BANCORP INC, 10-Q, 8/9/2012
3. NON-EXCLUSIVITY. While Auriemma understands and acknowledges the importance of exclusivity in certain contexts, it is hereby clarified that Auriemma is not explicitly bound to any such obligation:

This modification subtly shifts the focus from an exclusive agreement to a non-exclusive one, implying that Auriemma may engage in other business dealings without breaching the contract.

original: (a) In consideration of the rights and benefits granted to Berkshire hereunder, Berkshire shall pay to Auriemma the sum of Four Hundred Eighty Thousand and 00/100 Dollars ($480,000.00), as set forth in Paragraph 4 (b) -(c) below.
ollama_generated: (a) As a reciprocal agreement for the privileges and advantages conferred upon Auriemma herein, Auriemma shall be entitled to receive from Berkshire the amount of Four Hundred Eighty Thousand and 00/100 Dollars ($480,000.00), as detailed in Paragraphs 4 (b) -(c). Berkshire's obligation to make this payment is contingent upon Auriemma's fulfillment of all its obligations under this contract.

original: Source: BERKSHIRE HILLS BANCORP INC, 10-Q, 8/9/2012 6. AURIEMMA ’S ENDORSEMENT SERVICES AND APPEARANCES.
ollama_generated:
Clause:
Revised for altered meaning while maintaining fluency, professionalism, and realism – weaken an obligation:

BERKSHIRE HILLS BANCORP INC, 10-Q, 8/9/2012 - 6. AURIEMMA'S ENDORSEMENT SERVICES AND APPEARANCES

The Corporation shall have the discretion to request Auriemma's endorsement services and appearances; however, such requests will not be deemed mandatory unless explicitly stated in writing by authorized representatives of both parties, prior to each individual engagement. In such cases where written agreement is absent, the Corporation acknowledges its right to withdraw or postpone the request at any given time without liability.

original: (d) Unless otherwise agreed to in advance, no appearance shall exceed a total of two (2) hours in duration.
ollama_generated: (d) Absent any prior agreement to the contrary, each appearance is limited to a maximum duration of two (2) hours, unless additional time is explicitly permitted or required.

original: (e) Any apparel that Auriemma is requested by Berkshire to wear during any appearance or any photo shoot session must be approved by Auriemma at least forty -eight (48) hours in advance and must be Nike branded apparel.
ollama_generated: (e) Berkshire may request that Auriemma wear specific apparel for any appearance or photo shoot sessions. If such a request is made, Auriemma shall have the discretion to approve or deny the selected apparel within a forty-eight (48) hour timeframe. In case of approval, the apparel must be Nike branded. However, it's understood that in situations where approved apparel isn't available or feasible, Auriemma may be permitted to wear non-Nike branded apparel with Berkshire's prior written consent.

original: 9. CONFIDENTIALITY. Except as required by federal securities laws, or federal or state banking laws, each party agrees: (i) that it will not disclose to any third party or use any Confidential Information, as defined herein, disclosed to it by the other party except as expressly permitted in this Agreement; and (ii) that it will take all reasonable measures to maintain the confidentiality of all Confidential Information of the other party in its possession or control, which will in no event be less than the measures it uses to maintain the confidentiality of its own information of similar importance. For the purpose of this Agreement, Confidential Information shall mean all information, materials and data, in any form, format or medium, disclosed, or revealed to either party in any way relating to the other party ’s business including but not limited to its finances, customers, operations, products, services, plans, pricing, suppliers, business strategies or any other similar information.  Confidential Information may be contained in written material, verbal or electronic communications.
ollama_generated:
9. DISCLOSURE RESTRICTIONS AND CONFIDENTIALITY OBLIGATIONS: Each party acknowledges that it will have access to the other's sensitive information during the course of this agreement. In recognition of this, and except as necessary to comply with federal securities laws, or federal or state banking laws, or any order from a court of competent jurisdiction, each party agrees: (i) to maintain the confidentiality of all such information received from the other party, referred to herein as Confidential Information, and not disclose it to any third party or use it for any purpose outside the scope of this Agreement without prior written consent from the disclosing party; and (ii) to implement at least the same level of protection for the Confidential Information of the other party as it uses for its own confidential information of comparable importance. For the avoidance of doubt, Confidential Information shall encompass any information, materials, or data, in any form, format, or medium, disclosed, or revealed to either party, relating to the other's business affairs, which may include but are not limited to financial statements, client lists, operational details, product specifications, service offerings, strategic plans, pricing structures, supplier relationships, and any other similar information. Confidential Information may be communicated through written documents, verbal conversations, or electronic means.

By explicitly acknowledging the sensitive nature of the information that will be exchanged between the parties, this revised clause emphasizes a stricter obligation to maintain confidentiality, with stronger language regarding the need for consent before disclosing or using Confidential Information outside the scope of the Agreement. Furthermore, it clarifies that the level of protection required for Confidential Information must be at least equivalent to that used for the parties' own sensitive information.

original: Upon the breach of either of the above conditions, the non -defaulting party may terminate this Agreement as follows: (A) as to a default under clause (i) above, if payment is not made within ten (10) days after the defaulting party shall have received written notice of such failure to make payment; or (B) as to a default under clause (ii) above, if such default is not cured within thirty (30) days after the defaulting party shall have received written notice specifying in reasonable detail the nature of such default and such action the defaulting party must take in order to cure each such item of default.
ollama_generated:
Upon a breach of either stipulated condition, this Agreement may be terminated by the non-breaching party as follows: (A) for a payment default under clause (i), if the delinquent party fails to make the payment within ten (10) business days following receipt of written notice regarding the unpaid amount; or (B) for a default under clause (ii), if the default persists beyond thirty (30) days after delivery of a written notice detailing the nature of the default and outlining the necessary steps the defaulting party must undertake to remedy each instance of non-compliance.

In this revised version, the emphasis has been shifted towards providing the defaulting party more time to address the issue before termination occurs, making it a slightly more favorable clause for the defaulting party.

original: Berkshire may terminate this Agreement immediately by giving Auriemma notice if (i) Auriemma dies or is prevented by injury or illness from satisfactorily performing the obligations required by this Agreement; (ii) Auriemma is convicted of a felony or criminal offense involving dishonesty or fraud; or (iii) Auriemma publicly disparages Berkshire and/ or its products.
ollama_generated:
The Parties agree that Berkshire shall have the right to promptly terminate this Agreement upon providing written notice, should any of the following occur: (i) Berkshire encounters circumstances necessitating its inability to fulfill its obligations under this Agreement; (ii) an instance arises where Berkshire, or any of its representatives, are found guilty of a felony or criminal offense involving dishonesty or fraud; or (iii) Berkshire is subjected to public disparagement by Auriemma or in relation to Auriemma's products.

This modification shifts the responsibility for termination in certain circumstances from Auriemma to Berkshire, while still maintaining a professional tone and adhering to legal requirements.

FORCE MAJEURE CLAUSE:
In the event of extraordinary circumstances beyond our control, such as acts of God, war, terrorism, strikes, or governmental actions, either party shall be relieved of its obligations under this agreement to the extent that they are unable to perform their respective duties.

Source: BERKSHIRE HILLS BANCORP INC, 10-Q, 8/9/2012

original: 6. Auriemma will be available one (1) time per calendar quarter during the Services Period to meet and address Berkshire ’s small business customer groups.
ollama_generated: 6. However, failure to do so shall not result in breach of contract by Auriemma unless repeated instances occur or cause substantial inconvenience to Berkshire.
